name: Slice & Bake Peanut Butter Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut four 14" x 12-inch (14 inches by 12 inches) pieces of waxed paper or plastic wrap and set aside.
2. Cream together shortening, sugar, brown sugar, peanut butter, and vanilla extract in a large bowl until light and fluffy.
3. Beat in eggs until combined.
4. In a separate bowl, mix together flour and baking soda.
5. Gradually stir the dry ingredients into the wet mixture until fully incorporated.
6. Divide the dough into four equal portions.
7. Shape each portion into an 8 to 10 inch (8 inches by 10 inches) roll using waxed paper or plastic wrap.
8. Place wrapped rolls in a freezer-safe container with a tight-fitting lid, or wrap them airtight in foil.
9. Label and date the container.
10. Store in the freezer for up to 6 months.
To make cookies:
1. Thaw dough slightly at room temperature.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
3. Cut dough into slices or chunks.
4. Roll each piece into a ball.
5. Place balls on an ungreased baking sheet about 1 1/2 inches apart.
6. Use fork tines to flatten cookies in a crisscross pattern.
7. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes until lightly browned around the edges.
8. Remove cookies from the baking sheet and cool on wire racks.
